feed.title: LIGHTNING ECONOMICS - The Bridge Between Bitcoin's Two Identities

entry.title: LIGHTNING ECONOMICS - The Bridge Between Bitcoin's Two Identities
entry.summary:
The Lightning Network presents a compelling opportunity for generating fee income through routing activities, characterized by an impressive capital turnover rate approximately seven times annually. This rate is on par with the most dynamic sectors in traditional finance. Remarkably, dedicated routing capital on the Lightning Network can achieve an 88-fold turnover without any leverage—a stark contrast to similar high-yield scenarios in banking that typically require ten times leverage. Operators who manage these systems professionally have reported achieving gross annualized returns of between 5 and 6%. It's important to note that within this framework, the bitcoin used as the deployed capital, transaction medium, and revenue remains under single custody without the need to relinquish property rights.
This efficient use of capital asset has been formalized in the concept of Lightning Routing Income (LRI), which has been recognized as the third category of Bitcoin treasury returns. Despite over 190 public companies holding Bitcoin, most of these holdings do not generate operational income under conventional financial strategies, which usually involve access to capital markets and a managed Net Asset Value above one, along with the ability to issue continuously. However, a significant observation is that at least 37 of the top 100 Bitcoin treasury firms are trading below their net asset value, indicating a prevalent market challenge.
The report titled "Lightning Economics" delves into how the Lightning Network can bridge the existing gap by facilitating returns through high capital velocity. It discusses why Bitcoin is particularly well-suited for this business model and outlines the necessary properties that must be maintained for yields to be considered native to the Bitcoin network. Additionally, it addresses concerns about infrastructure integrity and centralization within the network, evaluating whether its growth trajectory is sustainable and self-reinforcing. This comprehensive analysis incorporates insights from peer-reviewed economic research and first-party node operating data, alongside introducing the first published Return on Invested Capital (ROIC) framework specifically designed for capital deployed on the Lightning Network. For those interested in exploring this in-depth analysis, the full report is available for download at [ZeusLN](https://zeusln.com/lightning-economics).
